opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I read that my blood test means I have a fatal liver disease. I feel dizzy, my mind is completely racing, and my liver feels like it's swelling up right now.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I'm terrified my liver is failing right this minute and that I'm going to slip into a coma tonight — I need someone to check me urgently.

symptom_narrative.onset: tonight, after I spent hours reading online about a blood test result I got this week
symptom_narrative.duration: a few hours — it came on suddenly this evening
symptom_narrative.character: my heart is pounding and racing, I feel dizzy and strangely detached from everything, my mind won't stop, and there's a tight, gripping feeling under my right ribs like my liver is swelling
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: absolutely terrifying — it feels like I'm about to go into a liver coma
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my chest is pounding and there's a tight grip on the right side under my ribs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started this evening, and worse the more I read about it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; my spouse trying to talk me down helps a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: reading more about the condition online makes it much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pounding palpitations, dizziness, breathing very fast, and a feeling of being detached from reality; my eyes are not yellow, my urine is normal colour, my stools are normal, and I'm not actually vomiting
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've always been an anxious person about my health and I've had panicky moments before, but never anything as bad as this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sit still, can't sleep, and can't think about anything except my liver

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: told this week that a routine blood test showed an antibody that might mean a liver condition
past_medical_history.how_managed: no specialist review yet — I have an appointment to be arranged

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing tendency to worry a lot about my health

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with a supportive spouse who is here with me; I'm normally independent and manage everything myself

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading taken during acute distress on a consumer smartwatch, not a medical monitor
uncalibrated home cuff, taken while very anxious and hyperventilating

objective_data_offered.value: I'm breathing really fast and I can't seem to slow it down
visibly anxious and hyperventilating, holding the right side of her abdomen, but alert and talking in full sentences
spouse says she's been pacing and cannot stop talking about her liver, but she clearly knows where she is, what day it is and who we all are

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants absolute certainty that she is not dying of liver failure tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, repeatedly seeks reassurance and quotes things she has read online
